title: Postcolonial global justice [Book Review]
author: Chan (K.Y.S.)

opening_move.description: The review opens by identifying a gap between anticolonial political thought and global justice theory — two traditions that have rarely been put into conversation — and presents Chan's book as bridging them through an anticolonial egalitarian framework.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Global justice theory must be rethought as a project in decolonization, not just a matter of redistributive cosmopolitan principles

theoretical_positioning.description: The review presents Chan's synthesis appreciatively, contextualising the anticolonial-egalitarian framework as a productive bridge between two traditions.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: close reading of anticolonial and global justice texts

confidence_notes: Book review genre; primarily expository. Reviewer (Kai Yui Samuel Chan) shares surname with reviewed author (Shuk Ying Chan) — different people.
